subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of types of mixtures, and understanding of the processes of scientific ways of separating the components of mixtures
indicator_description: Identify types of mixtures by name and characteristics
examplars:
1. Group materials such as powder, pebbles, bottle tops, salt, sugar, sand, gari, gravel, oil, water and others into two main categories: solids and liquids. [cite: 815]
2. Put any two of the materials (in 1) together and describe the resultant nature of the product formed. [cite: 815]
3. Draw observable conclusions on homogeneous and heterogeneous characteristics from mixtures of two or more materials such as sand and gravel; sand and water; oil and water. [cite: 815]
4. Compare and contrast solutes and solvents based on their physical characteristics. [cite: 815]
5. Identify and separate mixtures such as sand and sugar mixture, sugar and salt mixture and solutions such as salt solution, sugar solution, fruit juice, vinegar solution based on their physical properties. [cite: 815]
6. Identify a suspension as a type of mixture e.g. mixture of groundnut paste and water in a glass. [cite: 815]
7. Differentiate between a colloid and a suspension and show the colloidal effect. [cite: 818]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of types of mixtures, and understanding of the processes of scientific ways of separating the components of mixtures
indicator_description: Design and perform processes for separating kinds of mixtures.
examplars: 1. Perform activities such as distilling, filtering, sieving and others to separate different kinds of mixtures and present a report on your findings using drawing and written work. [cite: 818]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of atoms and the atomic structure of elements in the periodic table
indicator_description: Describe atoms as composed of sub-atomic particles
examplars:
1. Explain an atom and its structure of an element using/linking it to the periodic table. [cite: 821]
2. List the sub-atomic particles found in the atom and indicate their location in the atom (e.g. proton, electron, neutron). [cite: 821]
3. State the electrical charges on the sub-atomic particles. [cite: 821]
4. Describe the differences between the atomic number and the mass number of elements. [cite: 821]
5. Determine the number of protons, neutrons and electrons in an atom. [cite: 821]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of atoms and the atomic structure of elements in the periodic table
indicator_description: Explain the arrangement of elements in terms of the number of protons in the nuclei of atoms of each element.
examplars:
1. Explain how elements are arranged in order of the number of protons using the periodic table. [cite: 824]
2. Draw the distribution of electrons (electron configuration) in the atoms. [cite: 824]
3. Explain the formation of ions. [cite: 824]
4. Describe a molecule as a combination of atoms. [cite: 824]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the types of cells and their structure in relation to different organisms
indicator_description: Examine and describe the structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
examplars:
1. Compare and contrast prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. [cite: 827]
2. Create a table to show a chart or a slideshow depicting images and labels of the types of cells. Identify their differences and similarities after observation. [cite: 827]
3. Draw and label a prokaryotic cell and a eukaryotic cell and make a presentation on what is observed. [cite: 827]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the types of cells and their structure in relation to different organisms
indicator_description: Classify organisms (plants or animals) as prokaryotic or eukaryotic based on the type of cells they are made of
examplars:
1. Observe and list examples of organisms; plants and animals as prokaryotic or eukaryotic based on each cell type. [cite: 827]
2. Explain the impact of prokaryotes and eukaryotes on humans health and devise safety measures to protect them. [cite: 827]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of the process of Carbon cycle as an example of repeated pattern of change in nature and how it relates to the environment
indicator_description: Explain the process of the carbon cycle.
examplars:
1. Identify the carbon cycle from the internet, charts or pictures and write short notes on what happens at each stage. [cite: 832]
2. Produce a flow chart to trace the process of the carbon cycle in nature. [cite: 832]
3. Explain the process of the carbon cycle depicting processes such as a) Photosynthesis b) Respiration c) Burning d) Decay. [cite: 832]
4. Compile information on the carbon cycle and give reasons why it is a repeated pattern e.g. it is because the carbon is circulated continuously in the environment. [cite: 832]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of the process of Carbon cycle as an example of repeated pattern of change in nature and how it relates to the environment
indicator_description: Describe the role of the carbon cycle to the environment.
examplars:
1. Describe the role of the carbon cycle in maintaining balance in the composition of air in the environment. [cite: 837]
2. Explain the effect of the carbon cycle on food chains, using diagrams. [cite: 837]
3. Describe the relationship between greenhouse gases and the carbon cycle. [cite: 837]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Demonstrate an activity to show the life cycle of the Anopheles mosquito and show how the effects of the mosquito on humans can be managed
indicator_description: Describe the life cycle and economic importance of the Anopheles mosquito
examplars:
1. Observe and draw the different stages of the life cycle of the Anopheles mosquito e.g. by breeding the mosquito in a glass jar. [cite: 840]
2. Describe the economic importance of the Anopheles mosquito. [cite: 840]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Demonstrate an activity to show the life cycle of the Anopheles mosquito and show how the effects of the mosquito on humans can be managed
indicator_description: Discuss the impact of the Anopheles mosquito on humans and how it can be controlled.
examplars:
1. Discuss the impact of the female Anopheles mosquito as a vector of plasmodium on humans. [cite: 840]
2. Generate solutions to control malaria in Ghana. [cite: 840]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills in planting crops on different seed beds.
indicator_description: Explore the different seed beds for planting crops in your community.
examplars:
1. Observe and discuss different seed beds for planting different crops. [cite: 845]
2. List and compare the differences and similarities among seed beds in the community. [cite: 845]
3. Match the types of seed beds with the types and stages of crops planted in your community. [cite: 845]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills in planting crops on different seed beds.
indicator_description: Plant different types of crops on different seed beds.
examplars:
1. Observe and discuss the practice of planting different crops in different seed beds. [cite: 845]
2. Select different plant parts, (seeds, seedlings, cuttings, leaves, roots) and plant them in different seed beds. [cite: 845]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.3.2 Demonstrate understanding of the differences in height, size, and flowering of crops grown in different seed beds
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the differences in height, size, and flowering of crops grown in different seed beds
examplars:
1. Measure the heights, sizes, number of flowers, and number of fruits of plants grown in different seed beds. [cite: 850]
2. Discuss the differences and similarities in the heights, sizes, number of flowers and fruits of plants grown in different seed beds using tables and graphs. [cite: 850]
3. Write and give presentations on the reasons for differences in the heights, sizes, number of flowers and fruits of plants grown in different seed beds. [cite: 850]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.4.1 Recognise the different types of feed for different types of animals
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the different types of feed for different types of animals
examplars:
1. Match the different types of feed with different types of animals. [cite: 853]
2. Discuss the types of nutrients and their sources in the different types of animal feed. [cite: 853]
3. Select and discuss appropriate feed for animal based on the proportions of nutrients indicated on the package or labels. [cite: 853]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.2.4.2 Demonstrate understanding of the importance of water and animal feed to the growth of animals.
indicator_description: Explain the importance of water and animal feed to the growth of animals
examplars:
1. List and discuss the usefulness of water and feed for the growth and reproduction of animals. [cite: 858]
2. Predict what will happen to animals who are not provided with adequate water. [cite: 858]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of parts of mammalian tooth and the functions of the different types of teeth in relation to feeding in man
indicator_description: Identify parts of a mammalian tooth
examplars:
1. Label parts, such as crown, neck, and root of a mammalian tooth. [cite: 862]
2. Explain the functions of each part of the mammalian tooth of humans. [cite: 863]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of parts of mammalian tooth and the functions of the different types of teeth in relation to feeding in man
indicator_description: Discuss the functions of the different types of teeth such as incisors, canines, premolars, and molars.
examplars:
1. Discuss the functions of the different types of human teeth. [cite: 866]
2. Draw the different types of teeth. [cite: 867]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of parts of mammalian tooth and the functions of the different types of teeth in relation to feeding in man
indicator_description: Explain the causes and prevention of tooth and gum decay.
examplars:
1. Describe the causes of tooth decay, gum diseases and formation of plaque and the proper way of preventing tooth decay. [cite: 869]
2. Demonstrate proper ways of cleaning the teeth. [cite: 870]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the outer planets of the solar system
indicator_description: Identify the outer planets of the solar system and describe their properties
examplars:
1. Describe the composition of the solar system using charts, pictures and digital content. [cite: 871]
2. Identify and draw the planets that form the outer solar system. [cite: 872]
3. Discuss the properties that are peculiar to each of the planet: Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. [cite: 873]
4. Search and explain why there is no life on Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. [cite: 874]
5. Construct a model of the outer solar system (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune) and display it for discussion. [cite: 875]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the interdependence of organisms in an ecosystem and their interaction
indicator_description: Explore the feeding relationships within an ecosystem
examplars:
1. Discuss how life on earth will be like without the sun. [cite: 877]
2. Explain the terms: producer, primary consumer, secondary consumer, food chain and food web as applied in energy transfer in an ecosystem. [cite: 878]
3. Illustrate with diagrams how energy from the sun flows through a food chain and food web in an ecosystem. [cite: 879]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of the different crop, animal and land combinations under various farming systems
indicator_description: Identify and describe the types of crops, animals and land combinations for the different farming systems
examplars:
1. Describe the types of crops, animals and land combinations in the different farming systems in your community. [cite: 881]
2. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of each farming system identified. [cite: 882]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.3.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of the different crop, animal and land combinations under various farming systems
indicator_description: Discuss the usefulness of the different crops and animals involved in the different farming systems.
examplars:
1. Explain how the different components of farming systems contribute to each other. [cite: 884]
2. Discuss and write down the contributions of crops and animals towards the sustainability of each farming system. [cite: 885]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Demonstrate the skill to evaluate the conversion of energy from one form to another
indicator_description: Describe energy conversion
examplars: 1. Describe how energy is converted from one form to another. [cite: 887]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Demonstrate the skill to evaluate the conversion of energy from one form to another
indicator_description: Discuss the importance of conversion of energy.
examplars:
1. Explain the processes that a dammed river goes through to produce electricity. [cite: 889]
2. Describe how to harness natural forms of energy into other forms. [cite: 890]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.1.2 Show an understanding of the sources of renewable energy and how to manage these sources in a sustainable manner
indicator_description: Describe renewable and non-renewable forms of energy
examplars:
1. Explain renewable and non-renewable sources of energy. [cite: 892]
2. Identify the various sources of renewable and non-renewable forms of energy and classify them e.g. wind, coal, hydro, crude oil, natural gas, solar and biogas. [cite: 893]
3. Describe how to produce energy from a renewable source. [cite: 894]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.1.2 Show an understanding of the sources of renewable energy and how to manage these sources in a sustainable manner
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to manage sources of renewable energy sustainably.
examplars:
1. Research about information on the stages involved in managing renewable energy sources. [cite: 896]
2. Create a table to describe challenges associated with the management of different sources of renewable energy. [cite: 897]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between heat and temperature.
indicator_description: Discuss the differences and the relationship between heat and temperature in the environment.
examplars:
1. Create a table to show the distinguishing features of temperature and heat. [cite: 901]
2. Discuss the relationship between temperature and heat. [cite: 902]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of electricity transmission
indicator_description: Explain how electricity transmission occurs.
examplars:
1. Identify different stages of electricity transmission. [cite: 904]
2. Draw a flow chart to show the stages of electricity transmission from the point of generation to the point of consumption. [cite: 904]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of the functions of capacitors in relation to LEDs, Diodes and resistors in electronic circuits
indicator_description: Demonstrate the charging and discharging action of a capacitor in a DC electronic circuit
examplars:
1. Research information about capacitors in electronic circuits and explain their functions when connected with direct current (DC). [cite: 905]
2. Describe the charging and discharging actions of a capacitor and explain the role of LEDs, diodes and resistors in an electronic circuit. [cite: 906]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.3.1 Evaluate the impact of conversion of energy and energy conservation on the environment
indicator_description: Explain the importance of conversion of energy and energy conservation in daily life
examplars:
1. Classify the importance of energy conversion and energy conservation in daily life. [cite: 909]
2. Search from multimedia sources, books, internet for information on the impact of energy. [cite: 910]
3. Conversion and conservation in their environment, and make a poster presentation on their findings. [cite: 911]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.4.1 Demonstrate the production of magnet, domestic and industrial application of Magnetic force and its relationship with Newton’s Second law of motion and in everyday life
indicator_description: Demonstrate simple ways of making magnets and show how magnetic force can be applied in domestic and industrial activities
examplars:
1. Produce magnets (using magnetic materials such as pieces of iron and bar magnet; and electricity). [cite: 913]
2. Demonstrate some application of magnetic force in domestic and industrial activities (E. g. compass, alarms, loud speakers, etc.). [cite: 915]
3. Explore other industrial and domestic applications of magnetic force and present findings. [cite: 916]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.4.1 Demonstrate the production of magnet, domestic and industrial application of Magnetic force and its relationship with Newton’s Second law of motion and in everyday life
indicator_description: Explain the relationship between magnetic force and Newton’s Second Law of motion; and show the law’s application to life.
examplars:
1. Explain Newton’s Second Law of motion with examples from daily life. [cite: 920]
2. Perform an experiment to show the relationship between force and motion using magnetic force, and the principle of Newton’s Second Law of Motion. [cite: 921]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.4.2 Demonstrate understanding of complex machines and how they work
indicator_description: Identify complex machines and describe their functions in life
examplars:
1. Recap what simple machines are from B7.4.4.2.1 [cite: 923]
2. Explain what complex machines are and show how different they are from simple machines. [cite: 923]
3. Identify simple machine in complex machines. [cite: 924]
4. Explain how the functions of a complex machine can improve the quality of life. [cite: 924]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.5.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills in the use of basic and simple agricultural tools for basic on-farm activities
indicator_description: Show and discuss the use of basic and simple agricultural tools for basic on-farm activities
examplars:
1. Collect and list different types of agricultural tools used for on-farm activities. [cite: 926]
2. Match each tool with the familiar type of agricultural activity it is used for and create an album of the tools. [cite: 927]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.4.5.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills in the use of basic and simple agricultural tools for basic on-farm activities
indicator_description: Engage in the use of basic and simple agricultural tools for basic farm activities.
examplars:
1. Explain how the different agricultural tools are used on a farm or school garden to perform specific agricultural activities. [cite: 929]
2. Practice the use of different agricultural tools for specific activities on a farm or school garden. [cite: 930]
3. Select appropriate tools for specific agriculture tasks. [cite: 931]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of waste management systems and apply it in an environment
indicator_description: Explain sustainable waste management practices
examplars:
1. Outline approaches to waste management in promoting sustainable management. [cite: 932]
2. Conduct a survey in a community’s waste management practices and present a report. [cite: 933]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of waste management systems and apply it in an environment
indicator_description: Apply knowledge of waste management practices to manage waste in a community
examplars:
1. Carry out an activity to manage waste using knowledge acquired in indicator B8.5.1.1.1 in their communities. [cite: 935]
2. Evaluate the waste management practices carried out in a community and present a report. [cite: 936]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of common communicable diseases, such as Hepatitis, of humans, causes, symptoms, effects and their prevention
indicator_description: Explain the symptoms, effects and prevention of common communicable diseases.
examplars:
1. Compile data on the number of males and females who suffer from common communicable diseases such as hepatitis, from a medical centre and determine the possible causes of these diseases. [cite: 940]
2. Identify causes, symptoms, effects and prevention of hepatitis, HIV, measles and others and make a presentation. [cite: 941]
3. Search for the causes, symptoms and prevention of hepatitis and develop a plan to minimise the disease. [cite: 942]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of common communicable diseases, such as Hepatitis, of humans, causes, symptoms, effects and their prevention
indicator_description: Analyse the risk factors of communicable diseases
examplars:
1. Search for information that is associated with communicable diseases. [cite: 945]
2. Create awareness about risk factors of communicable diseases such as hepatitis, HIV, measles and others in order to prevent the diseases in their schools and communities. [cite: 946]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.2.2 Demonstrate knowledge of the nature of selected bacterial diseases of humans, their causes, symptoms, effects and prevention
indicator_description: Explain the nature of bacterial diseases with special emphasis on food poisoning/gonorrhoea/ meningitis their causes, symptoms, effects on humans and prevention
examplars:
1. Discuss the nature of bacterial diseases. [cite: 949]
2. Search for information and make presentations on food poisoning, gonorrhoea, and meningitis diseases their mode of transmission from person to person, community to community and from country to country. [cite: 950]
3. Describe the symptoms, effects and prevention of food poisoning,, gonorrhoea, and meningitis diseases. [cite: 951]
4. Describe the role of individuals, community members and government in managing food poisoning, gonorrhoea, and meningitis diseases. [cite: 952]
5. Design and produce a poster to educate their community members on the incidence and control of named bacterial diseases: food poisoning, gonorrhoea, and meningitis. [cite: 953]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.3. 1 Demonstrate an understanding of connections among science, technology, innovation, society and the environment
indicator_description: Examine the relationship among science, technology, innovation and society.
examplars:
1. Explain the interrelationship of science and technology and innovation. [cite: 960]
2. Discuss technological advancements in the world and its impact on the Ghanaian environment. [cite: 961]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the effects of climate change in the world and greening of other tropical countries including Ghana.
indicator_description: Explain the concept of climate change and its effect on the environment.
examplars:
1. Describe the signs of climate change. [cite: 965]
2. Search for causes and effects of climate change and present a report. [cite: 965]
3. Explain how countries in the continents are adapting to climate change for example tree planting and legislation on bush burning. [cite: 966]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the effects of climate change in the world and greening of other tropical countries including Ghana.
indicator_description: Describe climate change and green economy actions.
examplars:
1. Describe climate change adaptation measures that can be applied in the community. [cite: 970]
2. Discuss mitigation strategies that your community can adapt to reduce the effects of climate change. [cite: 971]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of the differences among soils, plant roots, stems, leaves, flowers, and fruits of plants in the different environments
indicator_description: Discuss physical properties of soils
examplars:
1. Collect and describe different samples of soils (sandy soil, loamy soil, clay soil, etc.) from the school garden and the community. [cite: 974]
2. Discuss how each soil type retains water and supports the root system of plants. [cite: 975]
3. Conduct an experiment to demonstrate how different soil types retain water to support the root system of crops. [cite: 976]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of the differences among soils, plant roots, stems, leaves, flowers, and fruits of plants in the different environments
indicator_description: Analyse the physical properties of soils and soil water content and demonstrate their importance in crop production.
examplars:
1. Examine and discuss the different physical properties of each soil type and how these properties help support crop production. [cite: 979]
2. Observe and describe the growth of different plants on different soil types. [cite: 980]
3. Demonstrate how plants absorb water and nutrients from the soil (osmosis). [cite: 981]

subject: Science
content_standard: B8.5.6.1 Recognise the different types of rocks as origin of different types of soils
indicator_description: Observe and describe different types of rocks as origins of soils.
examplars:
1. Identify different labelled samples of rocks presented in the classroom/laboratory. [cite: 984]
2. Describe the visible characteristics of each rock. [cite: 985]
3. Collect samples of rocks from around the community and label them rock identification guide and compare them with the labelled laboratory samples in Exemplar 1. [cite: 985]
4. Research and report the stages of weathering of rocks to form soil. [cite: 985]
